user.title: Particle Man
user.description: Particle Man uses Google's air quality, pollen, and weather data. You'll need a Google Maps Platform API key with the Air Quality, Pollen, and Weather APIs enabled. [Get an API key →](https://mnestrud.github.io/particle-man/)

data_description.api_key: Paste your Google Maps Platform key here — it won't be visible after saving. [How to get a key](https://mnestrud.github.io/particle-man/)
data_description.latitude: Decimal latitude of the location to monitor. Pre-filled from Home Assistant.
data_description.longitude: Decimal longitude of the location to monitor. Pre-filled from Home Assistant.

error.invalid_auth: API key not recognized — make sure the Air Quality, Pollen, and Weather APIs are enabled in your Google Cloud project.
error.cannot_connect: Couldn't reach Google. Check your internet connection and try again.
error.unknown: Something went wrong. Check the Home Assistant logs for details.

abort.already_configured: A location at these coordinates is already configured.

init.title: Polling & Limits
init.description: Controls how often Particle Man checks for new data. {usage_summary}

data_description.latitude: Decimal latitude of the location to monitor.
data_description.longitude: Decimal longitude of the location to monitor.
data_description.update_interval: How often to fetch new data. More frequent checks are more responsive but use more of your monthly quota. [How call counts work](https://mnestrud.github.io/particle-man/api-usage/)
data_description.enforce_limits: When on, each API pauses for the rest of the month once its free quota is reached. Turn off to set your own limits on the last page. [Free tier details](https://mnestrud.github.io/particle-man/api-usage/)

apis.title: Data Sources
apis.description: Choose which types of data to collect for this location. Each is independent. [See what each provides →](https://mnestrud.github.io/particle-man/sensors/)

data_description.enable_air_quality: Current AQI, pollutant levels (PM2.5, ozone, NO₂, and more), and multi-day forecasts.
data_description.enable_pollen: Pollen index by type (grass, tree, weed) with forecasts and optional per-plant-species detail.
data_description.enable_weather: Current conditions, hourly and daily forecasts, and active weather alerts.

air_quality.title: Air Quality Options
air_quality.description: Customize what the air quality sensors report.

data_description.forecast_days: How many days of forecast data to include on each sensor (1 = today only, 5 = maximum).
data_description.language_code: Language for pollutant names and health guidance — use a two-letter code like en, es, or fr.
data_description.enable_local_aqi: Many regions use their own AQI scale alongside the universal one (e.g., US AQI). Enable this to get both.
data_description.local_aqi_code: The AQI standard used in your area. US users should typically choose US AQI. [Regional standards reference](https://mnestrud.github.io/particle-man/configuration/)
data_description.include_health_recommendations: Adds activity recommendations for different groups (general population, children, elderly, athletes) as attributes on AQI and pollen sensors.

pollen.title: Pollen Options
pollen.description: Customize pollen sensors for this location.

data_description.include_plant_sensors: Creates a separate sensor for each plant species in your area (e.g., Oak, Ragweed, Timothy Grass). Adds roughly 10–15 entities — useful for targeted automations or allergy tracking.
data_description.include_plant_descriptions: Adds botanical details (family, genus, typical season, known cross-reactions) as attributes on each plant sensor. Only relevant if individual plant sensors are enabled.

weather.title: Weather Options
weather.description: Customize the weather entity for this location.

data_description.weather_units: Temperature and wind speed units for raw sensor values. Home Assistant can convert units for display independently.
data_description.enable_weather_alerts: Creates a sensor showing active weather warnings for this area — tornadoes, floods, winter storms, and similar. Shows 0 when no alerts are active.

api_limits.title: Custom Limits & Quiet Hours
api_limits.description: {usage_summary} Each API pauses for the rest of the month when its limit is reached. [How to view current usage](https://mnestrud.github.io/particle-man/api-usage/)

data_description.aq_monthly_limit: Maximum Air Quality checks per month. Google's free tier is 5,000 — set higher if you're on a paid plan. [Pricing details](https://mnestrud.github.io/particle-man/api-usage/)
data_description.pollen_monthly_limit: Maximum Pollen checks per month. Google's free tier is 5,000.
data_description.weather_monthly_limit: Maximum Weather checks per month. Google's free tier is 10,000.
data_description.quiet_hours_enabled: Skip data checks during a set time window — useful overnight when the data isn't needed.
data_description.quiet_start: Stop checking at this time each day.
data_description.quiet_end: Resume checking at this time. If this is earlier than the start time, the pause spans midnight.
